comment.body:
the main thought: you don't need to try to trade every wave.
give it some time to show you what it wants to do. 
and only when you see it/feel it, and have a bias about the next move, trade it.

comment.body:
so  what i mean is that typicall mistake is when traders don't give it a time, but jump in a trade when they see the market moving in a direction. 
why? bcs it feels like you own the move, but in reality you are lagging already. 
we don't trade moves that are happening, we trade underpriced opportunities. frontrunning the market

comment.body:
A couple of questions:  
1. spot down / gamma down. doesn't that mean when spot goes down market makers have to sell spot because they are short gamma? 
2. I'm confused because you also mention it's a short butterfly which is mean reverting, so if the 5900
is at the center. 
3. why does vol of vol decreasing mean that traders are rolling their vix positions into March Opex? 
4. A short butterfly is this right: https://x.com/alma18499/status/1877487585917813037/photo/4
5. so as we go to 5900 it's net positive charm convexity and net negative vanna convexity. In a net negative vanna convexity MMs have to sell futures to hedge their position right? 
6. Given all of this I don't understand why 5945/25 is sticky.

comment.body:
i answer this one by one
1) spot down/gamma down means that as you go down on the chart MM gamma exposure decreases (shorter gamma, not necessary negative, but less and less gamma). 
This means that they have to buyback less and less as price falls , so it meets smaller counterforce, expandin volatility. 
they sell only, if it goes through a zero gamma line, but as you can see on the chart, there are multiple negative and positive gamma exposures. Dealers hedge their whole books, so we better look at it like what is the net gamma at some point. it is sitll positive above 5915
comment.raw_body:

they sell only, if it goes through a zero gamma line, but as you can see on the chart, there are multiple negative and positive gamma exposures. Dealers hedge their whole books, so we better look at it like what is the net gamma at some point. it is sitll positive above 5915

comment.body:
2) this is short fly. If it is mean reverting or not, depends on where we are. 
if we are in the center, then it rangebounds between the two long gamma strike around the center. 
but we are at the edge of the right wing of it, this is why pivot matters, like an "event horizont" that would suck the momentum towards the center.

comment.body:
zero speed isn't always the pivot. also note that this is a net weekly. 
it is more like the center, helps us indentify where we can expect shifts in the flows, nothing more. but flows doesn't shift aggressively. 
the pivot i coded is more complex: it is coded from a simulated dealer book breakeven + taking the speed curve into account, to catch the point where dealer hedging actually expected to shift
comment.raw_body:
zero speed isn't always the pivot. also note that this is a net weekly. 
it is more like the center, helps us indentify where we can expect shifts in the flows, nothing more. but flows doesn't shift aggressively. 
the pivot i coded is more complex: it is coded from a simulated dealer book breakeven + taking the speed curve into account, to catch the point where dealer hedging actually expected to shift
